series_title: Last Player Leo: The Dead Channel Arc
series_goal: Ten connected Shorts that still work standalone, following Leo as a dead friend's account pulls him through haunted calls, cursed streams, and a final choice where he deletes The Dead Channel, survives, and leaves one clean open thread for season two.

entries.theme: my dead friend joined voice chat at 3:07 AM
entries.story_tone: emotional digital horror, immediate hook, grief turning into dread
entries.arc_note: Inciting incident: Leo hears his dead friend Mason warn him not to answer the bedroom door.
entries.style_bible: Vertical found-footage gaming horror, realistic human Leo in a dark hoodie and headset, monitor glow on his face, readable emotions, bedroom details, no gore, the supernatural presence appears only as chat glitches, reflections, and audio waveforms. End with the username MASON typing: do not trust the replay.

entries.theme: the lobby showed tomorrow's death replay
entries.story_tone: fast-paced, escalating, creepy payoff, clear twist by second 25
entries.arc_note: Leo follows Mason's warning and finds a replay file dated tomorrow with Leo already in it.
entries.style_bible: Clean readable mobile framing, gamer bedroom at night, human Leo, red error lights, screen artifacts, replay timeline UI, practical room details, strong first-frame silhouette. Include a recurring red timestamp: 3:07 AM.

entries.theme: a stranger donated one dollar with Leo's home address
entries.story_tone: paranoid livestream horror, grounded, invasive, one impossible message driving the plot
entries.arc_note: The haunted signal moves from private voice chat into Leo's public stream, proving it can see the real room.
entries.style_bible: Found-footage livestream overlay, chat messages, donation alert, Leo visibly human and scared, practical bedroom geography, screen glow, no gore. End with the donor name showing MASON_BUT_NOT_MASON.

entries.theme: the AI voice cloned Leo before he spoke
entries.story_tone: uncanny, tech paranoia, focused on voice identity and betrayal
entries.arc_note: Leo learns the entity can imitate voices and used Mason's voice to lure him.
entries.style_bible: Close readable waveform visuals, voice chat UI, Leo recording himself, duplicated captions appearing before he talks, subtle reflection mismatch behind him. Keep the monster offscreen.

entries.theme: Leo found a hidden channel streaming his bedroom from five minutes ahead
entries.arc_note: The curse reveals a future stream where Leo makes the wrong choice, giving him one chance to change it.
entries.style_bible: Split-screen livestream composition, future timestamp, messy bedroom continuity details, Leo comparing his real room to the stream, red buffering icon, grounded fear over jump scares.

entries.theme: every subscriber became a username in the haunted lobby
entries.story_tone: bigger mythology, eerie community horror, suspenseful but still personal
entries.arc_note: Leo realizes the curse is feeding on viewers and the old Fortnite subscribers are not the same audience anymore.
entries.style_bible: Dark game lobby UI, rows of usernames, viewer count reflected in Leo's eyes, cold blue shadows, readable phone/monitor text, no copyrighted game branding.

entries.theme: Mason's last upload had one frame Leo never saw
entries.story_tone: mystery reveal, emotional clue hunt, creepy but sincere
entries.arc_note: Leo scrubs Mason's final video and finds the first real clue: the entity is called The Dead Channel.
entries.style_bible: Video editor timeline, one-frame flash, Mason represented only through old chat/avatar artifacts, Leo lit by monitor glow, hidden text reading THE DEAD CHANNEL in the frame.

entries.theme: the dead channel asked Leo to upload one more short
entries.story_tone: temptation horror, creator anxiety, morally tense
entries.arc_note: The entity offers views and growth if Leo uploads a cursed short, forcing the channel-growth theme into the story.
entries.style_bible: YouTube Studio-like analytics without copying exact branding, impossible view spikes, Leo's face torn between hope and fear, red notification glow, comments appearing from accounts with no profile pictures.

entries.theme: Leo's best-performing video predicted who vanished next
entries.story_tone: dark escalation, algorithm horror, cause-and-effect reveal
entries.arc_note: Leo learns performance is the curse's signal: more views means the entity reaches more people.
entries.style_bible: Analytics graphs, haunted thumbnails, names disappearing from a friend list, Leo still human and emotionally readable, strong red/blue contrast, keep violence implied not shown.

entries.theme: Leo deleted the dead channel before it went live at 3:07 AM
entries.story_tone: finale, urgent, emotional, satisfying choice with an open-ended sting
entries.arc_note: Finale: Leo chooses people over views, deletes The Dead Channel before the scheduled upload finishes, survives the night, and sees one final clean open thread for season two.
entries.style_bible: Countdown to 3:07 AM, upload progress bar, Leo's hand over the delete button, Mason's final real message, screen reflections, practical bedroom stakes. Leo deletes the channel in time and survives; final frame shows a harmless-looking new invite labeled LAST PLAYER LEO for a season-two hook.
